mnemonics: Two-Phase LOCKing (2PL) for Transactions: Growing (Acquire Locks) & Shrinking (Release Locks) phases
keyPoints: Validation (Optimistic Concurrency Control) performs checks in a final validation phase before commit, assuming conflicts are rare.
Pessimistic Concurrency Control (e.g., Locking) prevents conflicts by acquiring locks before accessing data.
Timestamp Ordering assigns a unique timestamp to each transaction and uses it to serialize transaction execution.
commonMistakes: Confusing Validation (Optimistic) with Locking (Pessimistic) techniques and their use cases.
Assuming a read operation requires an exclusive (write) lock instead of a shared lock, hurting concurrency.
Incorrectly implementing the two-phase rule in locking protocols, which can compromise serializability.
